rationale: The citing paper uses Mazar et al. (2008) in the Experiment 1 discussion to interpret an observed behavioral pattern: participants with incentives to overclaim still showed restraint. It explicitly invokes Mazar et al.'s self-concept-based explanation that people limit dishonest gain to preserve an honest self-view. This is more than a background list citation because it provides a concrete mechanism for interpreting a key empirical result, even though it is not central to the Bayesian truth serum method itself.
relation_description: Weaver and Prelec draw on Mazar et al.'s self-concept maintenance account to explain why respondents did not fully exploit incentives to exaggerate recognition. The cited theory is used as an interpretive lens for the citing paper's observed partial dishonesty.
